help.title: ヘルプ
help.systemGuideDesc: Complete guide for using and configuring Adaptive Traffic Monitoring System

questions.question: What is Adaptive Traffic Monitoring?
questions.answer: Adaptive Traffic Monitoring is an intelligent traffic management system that uses IoT and AI to optimize vehicle flow at intersections in real-time.

questions.question: How to access the dashboard?
questions.answer: Login using your credentials, then you will be directed to the main dashboard displaying real-time statistics from all connected intersections.

questions.question: Who can use this system?
questions.answer: This system is used by traffic operators, system admins, and authorized parties who have access for traffic monitoring and management.

questions.question: How to view intersection details?
questions.answer: Click on the intersection card in dashboard or use the search bar to find a specific intersection. You can also access the Intersections page to view all intersections in grid format.

questions.question: What is Manual Override?
questions.answer: Manual Override allows operators to take control of traffic lights manually during emergencies or special situations requiring direct intervention.

questions.question: How to create reports?
questions.answer: Open the Reports page, click "Create New Report", select intersection, report type, priority, and fill in report details. Reports are saved immediately and accessible to the team.

questions.question: How to view historical data?
questions.answer: Open the Analytics page to view charts and historical data trends. You can filter by date, intersection, and data type.

questions.question: IoT sensor not connected, what should I do?
questions.answer: Check internet connection, restart IoT device, and ensure firmware is updated. If problem persists, contact technical team via Support menu.

questions.question: What is the sensor data collection interval?
questions.answer: Default interval is 5 seconds. You can change it in Settings > IoT & Sensor. Shorter intervals provide more real-time data but use more bandwidth.

questions.question: How to add new sensors?
questions.answer: Contact system admin to register new device ID in Azure IoT Hub. After registration, configure ESP32 with the provided connection string.

questions.question: Data not updating in real-time?
questions.answer: Refresh your browser page. If problem continues, check data collection interval settings in Settings > IoT & Sensor. Also ensure stable internet connection.

questions.question: Charts not showing in Analytics page?
questions.answer: Ensure there is historical data for the selected period. Try selecting a wider date range. If still problematic, clear browser cache and refresh page.

questions.question: Cannot login to system?
questions.answer: Ensure email and password are correct. If you forgot password, use "Forgot Password" feature. If still problematic, contact admin to reset your account.

questions.question: Notifications not appearing?
questions.answer: Check notification settings in Settings > Notifications. Ensure browser allows notifications from this application. For email notifications, check spam folder.

questions.question: How to change password?
questions.answer: Open Profile menu > Settings > Security. Enter old password, new password, and confirm new password. Click Save Changes.

questions.question: What is Two-Factor Authentication?
questions.answer: 2FA adds an extra security layer by requiring a verification code in addition to password when logging in. Highly recommended for admin accounts.

questions.question: How to logout from all devices?
questions.answer: Open Profile menu > Settings > Security > Active Sessions. Click "Logout" on each session you want to end, or use "Logout All Devices" button.

guideItems.title: Getting Started Guide
guideItems.description: Learn the basics of using Adaptive Traffic Monitoring

steps.title: Login to System
steps.content: Open the application and enter your email and password. If this is your first login, you will be asked to change the default password.

steps.title: Familiarize Dashboard
steps.content: Dashboard displays real-time statistics, intersection status, and latest alerts. Use sidebar to navigate to other pages.

steps.title: Monitor Intersections
steps.content: Click on intersection card to view complete details including vehicle volume, light status, and sensor data.

steps.title: Create Reports
steps.content: Use Reports page to create documentation of incidents, technical issues, or important observations.

guideItems.title: Contact Support
guideItems.description: Our team is ready to help 24/7

steps.title: Hardware Preparation
steps.content: ESP32 DevKit, HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor, jumper wires, breadboard, and 5V power supply.

steps.title: Install Arduino IDE
steps.content: Download and install Arduino IDE. Add ESP32 board manager and install required libraries.

steps.title: Upload Firmware
steps.content: Open traffic_sensor.ino file, adjust WiFi credentials and Azure IoT Hub connection string, then upload to ESP32.

steps.title: Testing & Calibration
steps.content: Monitor serial output to ensure sensor sends data to cloud. Perform calibration if needed.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: Meet the ASTRAEA development team
team.heroTitle: Building the Future of Smart Mobility.
team.heroSubtitle: Our engineers, designers, and analysts collaborate to create predictive and efficient traffic management solutions for smart cities.
team.footerDescription: Our team works with the highest security standards and data privacy protocols to ensure the ASTRAEA system is safe for all citizens.
team.joinDescription: We are constantly looking for innovators, engineers, and creative thinkers to help us revolutionize urban mobility.

landing.heroSubtitle: IoT Technology for more efficient traffic management
landing.heroDescription: ASTRAEA leverages the power of IoT and AI to automatically optimize traffic light duration based on actual vehicle density, reducing congestion by up to 40%.
landing.featuresSubtitle: Our comprehensive features are designed to handle the complexities of modern urban traffic with high precision.
landing.realtimeMonitoringDesc: Monitor 4 or more intersections directly through a centralized dashboard. Full visibility of road conditions every second.
landing.adaptiveAIDesc: Automatic green light timing adjustment. AI analyzes vehicle counts and dynamically prioritizes congested lanes.
landing.smartAnalyticsDesc: In-depth data reports. Historical traffic patterns to assist decision-makers in long-term city planning.
landing.failSafeModeDesc: Guaranteed safety. System automatically returns to safe default timing settings if IoT sensors experience connection disruptions.
landing.ourMission: Our Mission to Revolutionize Urban Mobility
landing.aboutDescription1: ASTRAEA is a smart traffic management system that integrates artificial intelligence with autonomous drone fleets. We believe technology can solve increasingly complex modern transportation challenges.
landing.aboutDescription2: Our platform is designed to help city governments and transportation authorities monitor, analyze, and optimize traffic flow in real-time, creating safer and more efficient roadways for everyone.
landing.teamSubtitle: Our dedication to creating technology solutions with real impact for society.

troubleshootingGuide.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshootingGuide.subtitle: Common problem solutions
troubleshootingGuide.mqttDisconnectedSolution1: Check EC2 status in AWS Console
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution1: Ensure SSID and password are correct
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution2: ESP32 only supports WiFi 2.4 GHz (not 5 GHz)
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution3: Distance from ESP32 to router < 10 meters
troubleshootingGuide.awsErrorCauseDesc: Wrong AWS credentials or no permissions
troubleshootingGuide.noDataCheck1: ESP32 connected to WiFi? (check Serial Monitor)
troubleshootingGuide.noDataCheck5: Dashboard connected to MQTT? (green status banner)
troubleshootingGuide.irSensor1: Check wiring: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, OUT → GPIO
troubleshootingGuide.irSensor2: Test with hand in front of sensor (LED should light up)
troubleshootingGuide.ultrasonicSensor1: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, TRIG → GPIO, ECHO → GPIO
troubleshootingGuide.ultrasonicSensor3: Ensure no obstacles in front of sensor during test
troubleshootingGuide.contactSupport: If the issue persists, contact technical support:
troubleshootingGuide.documentationDesc: For more detailed guide, please read the complete documentation files:

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content: ASTRAEA (Adaptive Traffic Light Monitoring System) is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information when using our system.

dataUsage.title: How We Use Information
items: Improve user experience and system functionality
Analyze and optimize traffic light performance

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.description: We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your data:

dataSharing.title: Information Sharing
dataSharing.description: We will not sell, rent, or share your personal information with third parties without your consent, except in the following situations:
items: Protecting the rights and security of ASTRAEA and its users
With trusted service providers (with confidentiality agreements)

cookies.title: Cookies and Tracking Technologies
cookies.description: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve user experience, analyze usage, and store your preferences. You can set your browser to reject cookies, but this may affect system functionality.

policyChanges.title: Policy Changes
policyChanges.description: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be informed via email or in-system notifications. Continued use after changes indicates your acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us:

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing and using ASTRAEA (Adaptive Traffic Light Monitoring System), you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree with these terms, please do not use our services.

definitions.title: Definitions
definitions.serviceDesc: Refers to the ASTRAEA platform and all features, functions, and content provided.
definitions.userDesc: Any individual or entity that registers and uses ASTRAEA services.
definitions.contentDesc: Data, information, text, graphics, and other materials displayed in the system.

userAccount.title: User Account
registrationItems: You must be at least 18 years old to create an account
Information provided must be accurate and complete
You are responsible for maintaining account credential confidentiality
One person is only allowed to have one account
userAccount.securityDesc: You are responsible for all activities that occur on your account. Immediately report to us if you suspect unauthorized access.

serviceUsage.title: Service Usage
permittedItems: Monitor and manage traffic light systems
Collaborate with team through notification features
prohibitedItems: Perform reverse engineering or system decompilation

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property Rights
intellectualProperty.description: All content, features, and functionality of ASTRAEA are protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws. You are not permitted to:
items: Copy or duplicate services without written permission
Use ASTRAEA logo or brand without approval
Create derivative works from our services

serviceAvailability.title: Service Availability
serviceAvailability.description: We strive to provide reliable services available 99.9% of the time. However, we do not guarantee that services will always be free from interruptions, delays, or errors. We reserve the right to perform scheduled maintenance and are not responsible for losses arising from unavoidable downtime.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
items: Services are provided "as is" without express or implied warranties
We are not responsible for direct, indirect, or consequential damages
Users are responsible for operational decisions based on system data
We are not responsible for data loss due to force majeure

termination.title: Service Termination
termination.description: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your access to services if:
items: You violate these Terms and Conditions
You provide false or misleading information

changes.title: Terms Changes
changes.description: We may modify these Terms and Conditions at any time. Material changes will be informed via email or in-system notifications at least 30 days before taking effect. Continued use after changes indicates your acceptance of the updated terms.

law.title: Governing Law
law.description: These Terms and Conditions are governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Indonesia. Any disputes arising will be resolved through the Central Jakarta District Court.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: If you have questions about these Terms & Conditions, please contact us:

helpCenter.title: Help Center
helpCenter.subtitle: Find answers to your questions about ASTRAEA

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started Guide
gettingStarted.description: Learn how to use ASTRAEA from scratch

whatIsAstraea.question: What is ASTRAEA?
whatIsAstraea.answer: ASTRAEA (Adaptive Traffic Light Monitoring System) is an IoT-based traffic light monitoring and management system that enables real-time monitoring and traffic data analytics.

howToRegister.question: How to register an account?
howToRegister.answer: Click the 'Register' button on the main page, fill out the form with required information, and verify your email. After admin approval, you can login and use the system.

mainFeatures.question: What are ASTRAEA's main features?
mainFeatures.answer: Main features include: real-time traffic light monitoring, analytics dashboard, intersection management, IoT device configuration, notification system, automated reports, and user management.

accessDashboard.question: How to access the dashboard?
accessDashboard.answer: After logging in, you will be automatically directed to the dashboard. Or click the 'Dashboard' button in the navbar to access it.

realTimeMonitoring.question: What is real-time monitoring?
realTimeMonitoring.answer: Real-time monitoring allows you to see traffic light status live using SignalR technology. Data is updated automatically without page refresh.

cannotLogin.question: Cannot login, what should I do?
cannotLogin.answer: Ensure your email and password are correct. If you forgot your password, use the 'Forgot Password' feature. If still problematic, contact the administrator.

dataNotUpdating.question: Data not updating in real-time?
dataNotUpdating.answer: Check your internet connection. If connection is good but still problematic, try refreshing the page or logout and login again.

deviceNotDetected.question: IoT device not detected?
deviceNotDetected.answer: Ensure the device is properly configured on the IoT Config page. Check device connection to Azure IoT Hub and device status on dashboard.

changeProfile.question: How to change profile?
changeProfile.answer: Click your profile picture in the navbar, select 'My Profile', then click the 'Edit' button to change profile information.

changePassword.question: How to change password?
changePassword.answer: On the profile page, select the 'Security' tab, enter old password and new password, then click 'Save Changes'.

roleDifference.question: What's the difference between Admin and Operator roles?
roleDifference.answer: Admin has full access to manage users, intersections, and system configuration. Operator can view data and monitoring but cannot change system configuration.

contactSupport.title: Need Further Assistance?
contactSupport.description: Our support team is ready to help you. Contact us through one of the channels below.
